items.name: Handala (Iran-linked)
items.targets: Stryker Corporation (US medical device manufacturer) - wiped 200K devices causing global network disruption; Raz Zimmt (head of Iran Desk at Israeli security institutes)
items.ttps: Wiper malware deployment (T1485 Data Destruction), supply chain attacks, remote device wiping, destructive operations targeting healthcare infrastructure

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Telus Digital (Canadian) - claims 1 petabyte data theft; massive data breach confirmed by company
items.ttps: Large-scale data exfiltration (T1020), Salesforce targeting, credential theft, data breach operations

items.name: Qilin Ransomware
items.targets: Syed Professional Services, TDS Construction Group, Silvon Software Group
items.ttps: Ransomware deployment (T1486), double extortion, data exfiltration prior to encryption

items.name: Akira Ransomware
items.ttps: Ransomware deployment (T1486), financial sector targeting, data encryption

items.name: Russian State-Sponsored Actors
items.targets: Canadian governments, businesses and military - per Canadian cyber spy agency warning
items.ttps: Espionage operations (T1590 Reconnaissance), government targeting, critical infrastructure attacks

items.name: Lazarus Group (North Korea-linked)
items.targets: Multiple targets mentioned in context of APT activity and Splunk RCE exploitation
items.ttps: APT operations, RCE exploitation, supply chain compromise, persistent access

items.name: Multiple Threat Actors
items.targets: Microsoft 365 users globally via OAuth device code abuse and Cloudflare anti-bot bypass
items.ttps: OAuth device code phishing (T1566.002), credential theft without password stealing, Cloudflare security bypass for credential harvesting (T1589.001)

canada_landscape.summary: Canadian organizations faced significant threats in the past 48 hours. Russian state-sponsored hackers are actively targeting Canadian governments, businesses, and military per cyber spy agency warnings. Two major data breaches hit Canadian companies: Telus Digital confirmed a massive breach after ShinyHunters claimed 1 petabyte of data theft, and Loblaw suffered a data breach exposing sensitive customer information. No CCCS advisories were identified in the reporting period.
canada_landscape.retailers: Loblaw suffered data breach exposing sensitive customer information
